title: Fix memory gateway write path — narrow exception handling in UpdatePageAsync
description:
From supplemental audit finding 3 (84% confidence) and deep_code_audit finding 3 (87%): RestMemoryGateway.UpdatePageAsync catches ALL exceptions when fetching the existing page, logs "404 or parse error", and then unconditionally issues a PUT upsert. Auth failures, timeouts, 500s all get flattened into the same recovery path.

Fix: Only catch HttpRequestException with 404 status code for the "page not found" fallback. Let other exceptions (auth, timeout, 500) propagate. This prevents silent conversion of infrastructure failures into unintended writes.

Also: CreatePageAsync accepts a `type` parameter that is never used. Either wire it into the request payload or remove the parameter.

Source: memorysmith_agent_supplemental_audit_findings_2026-06-25.md (Finding 3), deep_code_audit (Finding 3)
